meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no understanding of APIs (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Solve API Integration for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with API complexity. Learn how to use Claude Code and Antigravity to automate your backend integrations and ship professional SaaS apps faster.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be an API expert to build robust SaaS applications. By leveraging Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of documentation parsing and Antigravity to scaffold your boilerplate, you can abstract away API complexity, allowing you to focus on high-value features and rapid client delivery.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new project is where most freelancers lose their momentum. You have the vision, but the technical wall of 'API documentation' feels like an unscalable mountain. Hours spent digging through cryptic JSON schemas and authentication flows are hours stolen from your bottom line and your sleep.

But the era of manual API orchestration is over. With Claude Code and Antigravity, you are no longer just a coder; you are an architect of automation. We are going to bridge the gap between 'I don't understand APIs' and 'I just shipped a production-grade integration' in less time than it takes to brew your morning coffee.

steps.title: Initialize the Workspace with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a clean, AI-ready directory structure that allows Claude Code to index your files effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional project scaffold for a Node.js API client, including .env management, error handling middleware, and a modular architecture.

steps.title: Contextualize the API Documentation
steps.description: Feed the raw API documentation into your workspace so Claude Code has the 'ground truth' to build against.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation text file. Identify the authentication requirements, base URL, and the core endpoints needed for a CRUD operation.

steps.title: Automate Authentication Logic
steps.description: Let Claude Code write the secure boilerplate for API keys and request headers, preventing common security oversights.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust authentication handler for this API. Ensure it uses environment variables for security and implements a 401 retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create an auth service that handles Bearer token injection into all outgoing requests.'

steps.title: Generate Type-Safe API Clients
steps.description: Ensure your code is resilient by generating type definitions based on the API response structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the API schema, generate TypeScript interfaces for the request and response objects to ensure end-to-end type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Generate types/api.ts based on the schema extracted from documentation.'

steps.title: Execute Integration Tests
steps.description: Validate the API connection immediately without writing manual test scripts from scratch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that mocks the API response and verifies the integration layer handles success and failure states gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Create a test file that validates the service layer connection to the API endpoint.'

pro_tips: Always use the 'claude add' command to include your environment's README; it helps the AI understand your existing architectural constraints.
When facing cryptic API errors, copy the raw error log into Claude Code and ask it to 'map this error to a specific line in my service layer'.
Use Antigravity's 'watch' mode to automatically update your documentation index whenever you modify local API specs.
Request Claude Code to 'write a summary comment' at the top of every generated file; it keeps your codebase readable for future maintenance.
If an API call is failing, ask Claude to 'generate a cURL command' to test the endpoint outside your code, isolating the issue from your logic.
content.next_steps: Now that your API integration is stable, focus on building the UI components that consume this data. Use the same Claude Code workflow to generate your frontend components to maintain full-stack consistency.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the documentation and start shipping the features your clients actually pay for. By mastering this workflow, you reclaim your time and scale your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
